architecture.summary: TG-LLM is a framework that teaches LLMs temporal reasoning by learning to translate natural-language text into temporal graph (TG) representations, a latent symbolic intermediate form. The LLM is fine-tuned on a synthetic dataset (TGQA) to perform text-to-TG translation, then learns to reason over these graphs via Chain-of-Thought bootstrapping. Information flows from text input → LLM translation to TG representation → LLM reasoning over TG structure → final temporal reasoning output.

biblio.title: Large Language Models Can Learn Temporal Reasoning

claims.statement: The capability of TG translation learned on the TGQA dataset can be transferred to other TR tasks and benchmarks

Chain-of-Thought bootstrapping and graph data augmentation strategies, which maintain a balance between usefulness and diversity, bring more reliable CoTs and final results than vanilla CoT distillation
Temporal reasoning presents a significant challenge for LLMs due to its reliance on diverse temporal concepts and intricate temporal logic
A temporal graph (TG) latent representation enhances the learning of temporal reasoning compared to reasoning over the original context
